what_it_does: Declares the INTERACTION mark-type 'reaction' as a module-level MARK_TYPE dictionary: an operator reaction-stamp on a rendered gallery element whose value is a reaction label and whose direction is 'surface'.
observations: The docstring frames the mark as the route-back of gallery:direction → territory_write → suite.mark
The allowed reaction labels (good/wrong/explain/remember_this/do_this) are described but only live in the docstring/taxonomies.json, not in code
The file contains no executable logic or code symbols beyond the MARK_TYPE constant
standouts: Explicit route-back lineage from gallery:direction through territory_write to suite.mark
Additive, registry-is-truth semantics for an operator-facing interaction mark
Requirement that id MUST equal the file stem ('reaction')
conventions: Module-level constant named MARK_TYPE holds the substrate definition
File stem matches the declared id
Docstring doubles as a design note explaining upstream/downstream relationships
concerns: Reaction labels are not validated or enumerated in this file
'registry-is-truth' is asserted but not enforced here
The id/file-stem coupling is stated as a rule but not checked
notes: This file is a substrate declaration: it registers the 'reaction' interaction mark-type and its surface-to-mark lineage.
questions: How are reaction labels validated against wildcard's taxonomies.json?
Where and how is the gallery:direction → territory_write → suite.mark route implemented?
What enforces 'registry-is-truth' and additive semantics for this mark-type?
purpose_vs_actual: matches: the docstring and MARK_TYPE together define a reaction-stamp mark-type with a label value and surface direction, exactly as described.
apparent_themes: Bidirectional traceability from UI gesture to suite mark
intention_signals: Establish a lightweight, declarative contract for operator reaction marks so gallery gestures can be recorded as additive registry entries with a known semantic path back to gallery direction.
novelty: The explicit 'route-back' lineage from gallery:direction through territory_write to suite.mark, plus the rule that id must equal the file stem.
contribution: A declarative record of the reaction mark-type that captures its value shape, direction, and semantic lineage, which a fused system can keep as a mark-type substrate definition.
summary_for_embedding: Defines the 'reaction' interaction mark-type as a surface-direction operator stamp with a label value, linking gallery direction to territory_write and suite.mark.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a registry-truth substrate record for operator reaction stamps so gallery-rendered elements can be marked and traced back through territory_write.
